inputs.label: Cutting Length
inputs.businessContext: Total length of material to be cut along the tool path. Based on ISO 8688-1 tool life testing standards.

inputs.label: Feed Rate
inputs.businessContext: Linear feed speed of the cutting tool. Typical values from machinery handbook (e.g., 0.1-0.5 mm/rev for steel).

inputs.label: Spindle Speed
inputs.businessContext: Rotational speed of the spindle. Determines cutting speed; standard ranges per material (e.g., 2000-6000 RPM for aluminum).

inputs.label: Number of Passes
inputs.businessContext: Number of cutting passes required to achieve final dimension. Based on depth of cut and material removal rate.

inputs.label: Rapid Traverse Rate
inputs.businessContext: Maximum speed for non-cutting movements (tool positioning). Typical machine specs: 10-30 m/min.

inputs.label: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.businessContext: Total distance traveled during rapid moves per cycle. Includes approach and retract.

inputs.label: Tool Change Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to change a tool. Typical automatic tool changer: 2-10 sec.

inputs.label: Number of Tool Changes
inputs.businessContext: Number of tool changes per cycle. Based on operation sequence.

inputs.label: Loading/Unloading Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to load and unload workpiece. Manual: 20-60 sec; automated: 5-15 sec.

inputs.label: Idle Time per Cycle
inputs.businessContext: Non-productive time per cycle (e.g., dwell, inspection). Lean manufacturing focus on reducing idle time.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Low: ±20% uncertainty; Medium: ±10%; High: ±5%. Based on Six Sigma data quality assessment.

suggestedActions: cuttingTime > 600 ? 'Increase feed rate or reduce number of passes' : ''
rapidTime > 30 ? 'Optimize tool path to reduce rapid distance' : ''
idleTime > 60 ? 'Implement SMED to reduce idle time' : ''
